summary: Audience‑tape of the historic first “Unbroken Chain” shows decent sound but a mixed performance – the moment is unforgettable while the rest of the show feels average.
review: The recording is an audience capture that many listeners find clear enough to enjoy, especially the thunderous crowd reaction to the debut of Unbroken Chain. Musically the night is a classic “1995‑era” experience: highlights include So Many Roads, Alabama Getaway, Crazy Fingers and a sweet Stella Blue outro, but the first set is generally regarded as weak and Jerry’s playing/vocals show signs of decline.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 reviews give a clear picture: the recording quality is acceptable (audience source, generally good), but the musical performance is split – historic moment and several strong songs versus a weak first set and noticeable decline in Jerry’s playing. The blend yields an overall mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers call it a nice FOB or “average audience quality”. Crowd noise is loud (especially on Unbroken Chain) but the tape remains listenable; no major drop‑outs are reported.

poor_songs: First set overall (not a specific song but the early half is described as weak)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The show is typical of mid‑90s Dead: an uneven first set, followed by a second set that contains a few solid jams. The historic Unbroken Chain breakout dominates crowd reaction and memory, lifting an otherwise average performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocals are praised on So Many Roads (near‑falsetto) but noted as shaky, with forgotten lyrics and declining guitar work; many feel he’s “on his last legs” yet still giving his all.
band_member_comments.Phil: Only occasional mentions – credited for insisting on learning Unbroken Chain and discussing the song’s rarity.
